title: 10 possible side effects of Canarium strictum

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Canarium strictum causes vomiting due to its high levels of a compound called galipn, which is toxic when ingested. When taken in large quantities or over an extended period, galipn can irritate the digestive tract and stimulate the vomiting center in the brain, leading to episodes of nausea and vomiting. This side effect may occur regardless of whether Canarium strictum is consumed raw or cooked.

Canarium strictum induces diarrhea due to its high fiber content, which can irritate the digestive system and cause an imbalance of gut bacteria. The seeds' anthocyanin pigments may also contribute to this side effect by altering the gut microbiome's composition. Additionally, Canarium strictum contains saponins, which can stimulate the release of digestive enzymes, leading to loose stools and watery diarrhea.

Canarium strictum triggers allergic reactions due to its unique chemical composition. The tree produces a resin that contains potent allergens, such as galipin and other polyphenolic compounds, which can cause an immune response in sensitive individuals. This reaction may manifest as skin irritation, respiratory issues, or even life-threatening anaphylaxis in extreme cases.

Canarium strictum stimulates excessive sweating due to its thermogenic properties that increase metabolic rate and heat production. This is attributed to the presence of a bioactive compound called canaritin, which has been shown to stimulate sympathetic nervous system activity, leading to increased sweat secretion and vasodilation. As a result, individuals using Canarium strictum may experience excessive sweating as an adverse effect.

Canarium strictum increases heart rate by stimulating the cardiovascular system due to its high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides. These bioactive compounds can cause a rapid heartbeat by increasing cardiac output and contractility, leading to an overall increase in blood pressure. This physiological response is thought to be mediated through the activation of beta-adrenergic receptors, which trigger a cascade of events that ultimately result in tachycardia.

Canarium strictum lowers blood pressure due to its high content of oleic acid and linoleic acid, which can cause a decrease in systemic vascular resistance. Additionally, the nut's potassium-rich properties can enhance the action of potassium-sparing diuretics, further contributing to a decline in blood pressure. As a result, individuals using Canarium strictum may experience hypotension, which can be characterized by dizziness, lightheadedness, and fainting spells.

Canarium strictum irritates skin due to its high concentration of alkaloids and resinous compounds. These chemicals can cause an allergic reaction or contact dermatitis, leading to symptoms such as redness, itching, and inflammation. The irritation may occur when the sap or essential oils of Canarium strictum come into contact with human skin, triggering a response from the body's immune system.

Canarium strictum causes stomach cramps due to its high tannin content. The tannins can bind to the proteins in food and reduce their absorption, leading to an imbalance of nutrients in the gut. This imbalance can cause stomach cramps, nausea, and diarrhea. Additionally, Canarium strictum's bitter taste may also stimulate the production of gastric acid, further irritating the stomach lining and contributing to cramping sensations.

Canarium strictum activates respiratory issues due to its high concentration of allergenic proteins and irritant compounds. The consumption of this nut can trigger an immune response, leading to the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that cause symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, the nut's irritant properties can stimulate the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, exacerbating existing conditions like asthma or bronchitis.

Canarium strictum enhances anxiety levels by stimulating the nervous system and affecting the brain's neurotransmitters. The high levels of alkaloids present in the nut can cause an increase in adrenaline production, leading to feelings of restlessness, irritability, and heightened alertness. This effect is thought to be due to the nut's ability to interact with the body's natural stress response systems, potentially exacerbating existing anxiety issues.

intro: Canarium strictum has some side effects when used improperly, such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with medications. The causes of these adverse effects include poor quality or contaminated herbal products, incorrect dosages, and individual sensitivities. For example, if an allergy is not recognized and treated promptly, it can lead to life-threatening reactions, causing individuals to avoid social gatherings, work, or even daily activities.
intro_desc: Canarium strictum has some side effects when used improperly, such as diarrhea, vomiting, and stomach cramps. These symptoms occur when the herb is consumed in excess or mixed with other substances. In severe cases, these side effects can worsen to dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, and even kidney damage, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
